{"id":"project-LICHEN-mugl.1","title":"Spec: Gateway coordination protocol","description":"Define gateway coordination protocol based on IETF building blocks.

## Location
New spec document: spec/08-gateway-coordination.md

## Building blocks

### RPL multi-instance (RFC 6550 §5)
- Each gateway is a DODAG root
- Same RPLInstanceID = cooperating gateways
- Nodes see unified DODAG, can have multiple parents

### 6TiSCH-lite scheduling
Adapt Minimal Scheduling Function (MSF) for LoRa:
- Slot negotiation via CoAP (not 6P, too complex)
- Gateway claims slots, broadcasts schedule
- Conflict resolution: lower gateway IID wins

### CoAP coordination messages
New resource: /.well-known/lichen-gw

| Method | Path | Function |
|--------|------|----------|
| GET | /slots | Current slot allocation |
| POST | /slots | Claim slots |
| GET | /channels | Channel ownership |
| POST | /handoff | Node handoff request |
| GET | /nodes | Node registry query |

## Trust models

### Closed federation
- CONFIG: shared secret (PSK)
- CoAP messages authenticated via OSCORE with PSK
- All gateways in deployment share same key

### Open federation  
- Gateways have Ed25519 identity (like nodes)
- CoAP messages signed with gateway key
- TOFU: first contact establishes trust
- Optional: PKI with gateway certificates

## Discovery

### Backbone discovery
- Multicast CoAP to ff02::1 on backbone
- GET /.well-known/lichen-gw/info
- Response: gateway IID, capabilities, slot map

### LoRa discovery (fallback)
- Gateway announces include GATEWAY flag
- Other gateways hear announces on LoRa
- Establishes radio-path coordination

## Slot coordination

### Superframe sync
- Gateways agree on superframe start time
- GPS-equipped: use GPS epoch
- Non-GPS: one gateway is time master, others sync

### Slot allocation
- Divide slots by gateway count
- Gateway N owns slots: N, N+G, N+2G, ... (interleaved)
- Or: contiguous blocks (simpler handoff)

### Conflict resolution
- Two gateways claim same slot → lower IID wins
- Loser picks next free slot

## Node handoff

When node moves from Gateway A to Gateway B:
1. Node sends DAO to B (new parent)
2. B sends handoff request to A via backbone
3. A releases node from its registry
4. B confirms handoff to node

## Backwards compatibility

- Single-gateway deployments: no coordination needed
- Old gateways without coordination: work standalone
- New gateways discover and coordinate if peers exist","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T19:34:35.199335Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T19:34:35.199335Z","labels":["spec","protocol","gateway","scalability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-mugl.1","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-mugl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T19:34:35.329315Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value:
{"notes":"## Requirement: Both Federation Modes MUST Be Supported

Implementations MUST support both modes. Neither is optional.

### Closed federation (PSK)
- MUST be supported for enterprise/single-operator deployments
- Simpler provisioning: one shared secret
- Lower barrier for small deployments

### Open federation (signatures)
- MUST be supported for multi-operator/community deployments
- Uses existing Ed25519 identity infrastructure
- Enables permissionless gateway participation

### Mode selection
- Per-gateway configuration, not per-mesh
- A gateway MAY participate in both modes simultaneously:
  - Closed federation with known peers (PSK)
  - Open federation with discovered peers (TOFU)
- Mixed mode allows gradual migration from closed to open

### Interoperability
- Closed-only gateway: coordinates only with PSK peers
- Open-only gateway: coordinates only with signature-verified peers
- Dual-mode gateway: coordinates with both

### Default
- No default mode mandated
- Deployment chooses based on trust requirements
- Example configs for both modes in documentation

### Non-goals
- No central authority or registry required for either mode
- No mandatory PKI (TOFU is sufficient for open mode)
- No blockchain or consensus mechanism"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-mugl.1","title":"Spec: Gateway coordination protocol","description":"Define gateway coordination protocol based on IETF building blocks.

## Location
New spec document: spec/08-gateway-coordination.md

## Building blocks

### RPL multi-instance (RFC 6550 §5)
- Each gateway is a DODAG root
- Same RPLInstanceID = cooperating gateways
- Nodes see unified DODAG, can have multiple parents

### 6TiSCH-lite scheduling
Adapt Minimal Scheduling Function (MSF) for LoRa:
- Slot negotiation via CoAP (not 6P, too complex)
- Gateway claims slots, broadcasts schedule
- Conflict resolution: lower gateway IID wins

### CoAP coordination messages
New resource: /.well-known/lichen-gw

| Method | Path | Function |
|--------|------|----------|
| GET | /slots | Current slot allocation |
| POST | /slots | Claim slots |
| GET | /channels | Channel ownership |
| POST | /handoff | Node handoff request |
| GET | /nodes | Node registry query |

## Trust models

### Closed federation
- CONFIG: shared secret (PSK)
- CoAP messages authenticated via OSCORE with PSK
- All gateways in deployment share same key

### Open federation  
- Gateways have Ed25519 identity (like nodes)
- CoAP messages signed with gateway key
- TOFU: first contact establishes trust
- Optional: PKI with gateway certificates

## Discovery

### Backbone discovery
- Multicast CoAP to ff02::1 on backbone
- GET /.well-known/lichen-gw/info
- Response: gateway IID, capabilities, slot map

### LoRa discovery (fallback)
- Gateway announces include GATEWAY flag
- Other gateways hear announces on LoRa
- Establishes radio-path coordination

## Slot coordination

### Superframe sync
- Gateways agree on superframe start time
- GPS-equipped: use GPS epoch
- Non-GPS: one gateway is time master, others sync

### Slot allocation
- Divide slots by gateway count
- Gateway N owns slots: N, N+G, N+2G, ... (interleaved)
- Or: contiguous blocks (simpler handoff)

### Conflict resolution
- Two gateways claim same slot → lower IID wins
- Loser picks next free slot

## Node handoff

When node moves from Gateway A to Gateway B:
1. Node sends DAO to B (new parent)
2. B sends handoff request to A via backbone
3. A releases node from its registry
4. B confirms handoff to node

## Backwards compatibility

- Single-gateway deployments: no coordination needed
- Old gateways without coordination: work standalone
- New gateways discover and coordinate if peers exist","notes":"Commit: d4fc90a02","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T19:34:35.199335Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-22T22:15:44.536469931Z","started_at":"2026-07-22T22:14:41.049345974Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-22T22:19:41.049345974Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-22T22:14:41.049345974Z","labels":["spec","protocol","gateway","scalability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-mugl.1","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-mugl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T19:34:35.329315Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
